zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane verch Gronwy Tudor於1220年12月20日出生在英國薩默塞特時，她的父親Gronwy ap Tudur28歲，母親Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur24歲。她於1242年6月25日與Thomas de Knowsley在英國藍卡爾可諾福斯利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1301年12月31日在英國藍卡爾可諾福斯利享年81歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane verch Gronwy Tudor于1220年12月20日出生在英国薩默塞特时，她的父亲Gronwy ap Tudur28岁，母亲Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur24岁。她于1242年6月25日与Thomas de Knowsley在英国藍卡爾可諾福斯利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1301年12月31日在英国藍卡爾可諾福斯利享年81岁。

nameMeanings.name: Tudor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Tudur, a derivative of British Celtic Toutorīx, literally ‘people’ + ‘king’. It has sometimes been taken as a Welsh equivalent of Latin Theodorus, from Greek Theodōros ‘gift of God’ (see Theodore ), for which the usual Welsh form is Tewdwr.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Tudor, a variant of Teodor (see Theodore ).

History: The Tudor royal family, English sovereigns from the accession of Henry VII in 1485 to the death of Elizabeth I in 1603, were of Welsh origin, being descended from Owain ap Maredudd ap Tudur, who held lands at Penmynydd in Anglesey.

variants.name: Theodore
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Serge, Henri, Pierre, Alide, Andre, Antoine, Benoit, Berthony, Chantal, Daphnee, Edouard, Guilene.

French and Mauritian (Théodore); West Indian (in Haiti, Martinique, and Guadeloupe Théodore); English and Welsh: from a personal name derived from Greek Theodōros, a compound of theos ‘God’ + dōron ‘gift’, which was relatively popular in the Middle Ages because of its auspicious meaning. The name was borne by several early Christian saints. There was considerable confusion with the ancient Germanic name Theodoric (see Terry ). In Wales, the personal name Theodore was also adopted as an approximation to the Welsh name Tewdwr (see Tudor ). In North America, this surname has absorbed various other European cognates and their derivatives, e.g. Greek Theodorakis , Macedonian Todorovski, and Serbian Todorović (see Todorovic ).

variants.name: Tedder
meanings: English (Surrey): from the Middle English personal name Thedere, Teddere, which is either from the Old English personal name Thēodhere (from thēod ‘people’ + here ‘army’) or from the cognate ancient Germanic personal name Theudher, which seems to have been adopted by the Normans. Alternatively, perhaps it may be an occupational name from Middle English teddere ‘one who teds’, i.e. a person whose occupation was to spread mown grass for drying.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Gronwy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name of uncertain derivation, borne in the Mabinogi by Goronwy the Staunch , Lord of Penllyn. He became the lover of the flower-maiden Blodeuedd and murdered her husband Lleu Llaw Gyffes , but Lleu was later restored to life and definitively dispatched Goronwy.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane verch Gronwy Tudor was born on 20 December 1220, in Somerset, England, her father, Gronwy ap Tudur, was 28 and her mother, Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur, was 24. She married Thomas de Knowsley on 25 June 1242, in Knowsley, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 31 December 1301, in Knowsley, Lancashire, England, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane verch Gronwy Tudor est née le 20 décembre 1220, à Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Gronwy ap Tudur, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Knowsley le 25 juin 1242, à Knowsley, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 31 décembre 1301, à Knowsley, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 81 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane verch Gronwy Tudor am 20. Dezember 1220 in Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gronwy ap Tudur 28 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur 24. Sie hat am 25. Juni 1242 in Knowsley, Lancashire, England Thomas de Knowsley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 31. Dezember 1301 im Alter von 81 Jahren in Knowsley, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane verch Gronwy Tudor è nata il il 20 dicembre 1220 nel Somerset, England, sua padre, Gronwy ap Tudur, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Knowsley il 25 giugno 1242, nel Knowsley, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 31 dicembre 1301, nel Knowsley, Lancashire, England, all'età di 81 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane verch Gronwy Tudorが1220年12月20日にイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父Gronwy ap Tudurは28歳，母Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudurは24歳であった。1242年6月25日にイギリスランカシュアイアノウスリーでThomas de Knowsleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1301年12月31日にイギリスランカシュアイアノウスリーで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane verch Gronwy Tudor(이)가 1220년 12월 20일 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gronwy ap Tudur(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1242년 6월 25일 영국 랭카실르 노우슬리에서 Thomas de Knowsley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1301년 12월 31일 영국 랭카실르 노우슬리에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.20 December 1220–31 December 1301: 20 de dezembro de 1220–31 de dezembro de 1301
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane verch Gronwy Tudor nasceu em 20 de dezembro de 1220, em Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gronwy ap Tudur, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Knowsley em 25 de junho de 1242, em Knowsley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 31 de dezembro de 1301, em Knowsley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 81 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane verch Gronwy Tudor родилась 20 декабря 1220 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Gronwy ap Tudur было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Knowsley 25 июня 1242 г. в местности ноусли, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 31 декабря 1301 г. в местности ноусли, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane verch Gronwy Tudor nació el 20 de diciembre de 1220, en Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Gronwy ap Tudur, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Mrs. Gronwy ap Tudur, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Thomas de Knowsley el 25 de junio de 1242, en Knowsley, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 31 de diciembre de 1301, en Knowsley, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 81 años.
